title: Zoho CRM Data, Branding Skill, and Account Limits Discussion
summary: Concerns about Zoho CRM database model and usage limits were discussed. The primary decision was to develop a minimal branding skill for immediate organizational deployment via Anthropic. Technical adjustments for account limits and configuration were also advised.

decisions.description: Develop a minimal branding skill for immediate organizational deployment.
Create a new GitHub repository for organizational skills management.
Michael Donovan to spin up a new project for all Straw-related work.
Michael Donovan to contact Harinder regarding Anthropic account usage limits.

actionItems.task: Create new GitHub repository for organization skills with basic structure.
Spin up a new project for all Straw work and migrate settings/skills.
Contact Harinder regarding current Anthropic account limitations and resources.
Update configuration files to increase max bash output to 90,000 characters.
Create a hook to monitor and reformat read line counts over 2,500.

tensions: Disconnected Zoho CRM tables causing data integrity concerns.
High costs and volatility of hardware pricing.
Hitting Anthropic account usage limits and unexpected charges.
Difficulty in identifying specific Anthropic account plans and limits.
Initial denial of Claude code bug by provider.
Risks of 'dangerously skip permissions' without proper guardrails.
Limitations of max lines in Claude even with increased context window.

contributions.description: Raised concerns about disconnected Zoho CRM tables (users, tasks, meetings, calls).
Proposed a systematic Zoho API solution for daily health checks and weekly reports.
Proposed an internal marketplace for skills integrating with Claude and Co-Work.
Suggested skills for Gamma, brand consistency, and a feedback mechanism.
Proposed starting with a basic branding skill defining minimal guidelines (names, colors).
Confirmed ability to distribute organizational skills via Anthropic admin account.
Proposed creating a new GitHub repository for skills to manage updates.
Shared insights from Distler on setting up guardrails for permissions.
Advised increasing 'max bash output' from 30,000 to 90,000 characters.
Suggested creating a hook to monitor and reformat read line counts over 2,500.
